# Introduction

<!-- Para 1: SR/MA as gold standard; time/resource burden -->
Systematic reviews and meta-analyses represent the highest level of evidence in the hierarchy of clinical research, forming the foundation for clinical practice guidelines, health policy decisions, and regulatory approvals.
However, the conduct of a rigorous SR/MA is resource-intensive, requiring an estimated 100 or more person-hours and 12 to 18 months from inception to publication.
<!-- TODO: cite Borah 2017, Tsafnat 2014 -->

<!-- Para 2: Existing automation tools and their scope -->
Several automation tools have emerged to address this burden.
ASReview and Rayyan employ active learning and crowd-based approaches for title and abstract screening.
Covidence extends coverage to data extraction.
RobotReviewer automates risk-of-bias assessment.
More recently, LLM-based tools such as otto-SR and TrialMind have demonstrated promising results in screening and extraction tasks.
<!-- TODO: cite van de Schoot 2021, Ouzzani 2016, Kellermeyer 2018, Marshall 2017, Blaizot 2022, Choi 2024 -->

<!-- Para 3: The gap -->
Despite these advances, no existing tool covers the complete SR/MA workflow.
Statistical analysis, manuscript generation, and publication quality assurance remain entirely manual processes.
A 2025 scoping review of 37 studies on LLMs for systematic reviews concluded that current tools are "not yet ready for standalone use," citing concerns about accuracy, reproducibility, and quality control.
<!-- TODO: cite Gartlehner 2025 -->

<!-- Para 4: Aim -->
To address this gap, we developed meta-pipe, an LLM-agent pipeline that orchestrates the complete SR/MA workflow from topic brainstorming (Stage 00) through submission-ready manuscript (Stage 10).
The pipeline integrates large language model capabilities with established statistical methods and automated quality gates, maintaining human oversight at critical decision points.
We demonstrate its application on a meta-analysis of immune checkpoint inhibitors in triple-negative breast cancer.
